Recursion is the process of a function (or set of functions) that calls itself.

runs.text: is the process of a function (or set of functions) that calls itself.

text_blocks.text: Recursion is really just a special name for calling the same function within itself

runs.text: Recursion is really just a special name for calling the same function within itself

text_blocks.text: Recursion ends at a base case.
I can easily do this with loops, but for the sake of an easy example, we can easily break this down

text_blocks.text: Recursion is the breakdown of a problem into smaller chunks, like Matryoshka dolls

runs.text: Recursion is the breakdown of a problem into smaller chunks, like

images.ai_description: Five traditional Russian nesting dolls with colorful floral designs displayed against a patterned textile background, illustrating unique cultural craftsmanship and artistic heritage.

text_blocks.text: How can we take the large problem and break it up into something smaller?

text_blocks.text: Structured – Introduces subroutines (i.e. functions) and well-defined looping structures

runs.text: Structured – Introduces subroutines (i.e. functions) and well-defined looping structures

text_blocks.text: Object-Oriented – Introduces classes and a philosophy about programming

runs.text: Object-Oriented – Introduces classes and a philosophy about programming

text_blocks.text: Object-Oriented Programming is as it sounds.  Assume everything can be modeled as an object.

runs.text: Object-Oriented Programming is as it sounds.  Assume everything can be modeled as an

text_blocks.text: A class is code that describes objects of that type

runs.text: is code that describes objects of that

text_blocks.text: An instance is a particular object of a given type

runs.text: is a particular object of a given type

text_blocks.text: A field is a property of an object

runs.text: is a property of an object

text_blocks.text: A method is a function that is provided by the class for an object of the class type

runs.text: is a function that is provided by the class for an object of the class type

text_blocks.text: Property or method is not dependent on the individual instantiated object, but rather the class as a whole

runs.text: Property or method is not dependent on the individual instantiated object, but rather the class as a whole

text_blocks.text: The property/method exists between instances; any change made to static fields are reflected through all instances of the class

text_blocks.text: Static methods can only manipulate static fields
